//SchemaVersion: Bump when fields change shape; loaders use this to migrate.
//LibraryRoot: Absolute path to the music library root. Album folders land at LibraryRoot/<Album Artist>/<Album> (Year)/.
//DriveLetter: Optical drive letter, e.g. 'D:'. Filled by setup/Register-Drive.ps1.
//DriveOffset: AccurateRip read offset in samples for this drive. Filled by setup/Register-Drive.ps1.
//contactAddress: MusicBrainz contact address -- email or URL. Required by the MusicBrainz API terms (https://musicbrainz.org/doc/MusicBrainz_API/Rate_Limiting) so they can reach out about misbehaving clients. Sent ONLY in the User-Agent header on requests to musicbrainz.org / db.cuetools.net / gnudb.org and never leaves the machine otherwise. Either an email address (you@example.com) or a URL (e.g. https://github.com/yourname) is acceptable per MusicBrainz policy. Empty by default; the WPF first-run editor (or setup/New-RipperConfig.ps1) prompts for it.
//OneDriveSyncTargetRoot: Phase 6.2: absolute path inside the user's OneDrive (or any local folder) where ripped albums get mirrored when 'OneDrive' is in SyncTargets. Pick the folder via setup/New-RipperConfig.ps1 (it pops a folder picker seeded at the OneDrive root detected from HKCU\Software\Microsoft\OneDrive\UserFolder). null = OneDrive sync target reports Failed (nothing to copy to).
//SynologyUnc: Phase 6.3: UNC path of the NAS share that the 'SynologyNAS' sync target mirrors albums onto (e.g. \\nas\music). Any UNC server works -- the name is historical. null = the SynologyNAS target reports Failed (nothing to copy to). If HasSynologyCredential=true, the share is mounted via New-SmbMapping for the duration of each sync using the DPAPI-stored credential; otherwise the sync uses ambient session credentials.
//SynologySyncReviewQueue: Reserved for a future _ReviewQueue mirroring opt-in. Currently unused by the Phase 6.3 SynologyNAS target (the sync framework runs per album and is invoked only on Library moves, not Review-Queue routings).
//HasSynologyCredential: Phase 6.3: true means a DPAPI-protected PSCredential is on disk in credentials.clixml. The SynologyNAS sync target loads it via Import-RipperCredential and mounts SynologyUnc with New-SmbMapping for the duration of each sync. Set to false to use ambient session credentials (e.g. when the NAS share is already mapped in Explorer with 'Remember credentials').
//MetadataProviders: Ordered list of disc-id metadata sources tried in priority order. Phase 5.2: MusicBrainz (curated, canonical) then CuetoolsDb (community-submitted) then GnuDb (legacy freedb/CDDB catalog, huge coverage of older and community-submitted discs). When two or more return matches, an extra synthesized 'Merged (...)' candidate is prepended (earlier providers win on conflict, later ones fill nulls). Available: 'MusicBrainz', 'CuetoolsDb', 'GnuDb'. Note: the 'Search by text...' fallback dialog also surfaces text-search-only providers (iTunesSearch, Deezer) regardless of this list.
//CoverArtProviders: Ordered list of cover-art sources. First non-empty bytes win. CoverArtArchive needs a release MBID (so really only fires for MB-derived candidates); iTunesSearch + Deezer fall back to artist+album text search. All three are free / no auth.
//EjectAfterRip: Phase 5.4: if true, eject the disc after the rip / review / cancel flow finishes. Default true preserves the parent-friendly batch behaviour. The confirm dialog also exposes a per-rip checkbox seeded from this value.
//ContinuousMode: Phase 5.7: if true, MusicRipper keeps running after each disc and shows a between-discs dialog (Rip Next / Quit). Inserting a new disc auto-selects Rip Next. Default true so a parent can rip a stack of CDs without re-launching (one UAC prompt per session). Set false to restore the one-disc-per-launch flow.
//SyncTargets: Phase 6.1+: ordered list of sync-target names to invoke per album after a successful Library move. Each name maps to a function 'Invoke-RipperSyncTo<Name>' loaded from src/sync/. Built-ins: 'Stub' (writes a marker file under .musicripper/stub-sync/, used for testing), 'OneDrive' (Phase 6.2: robocopy mirror into OneDriveSyncTargetRoot), and 'SynologyNAS' (Phase 6.3: robocopy mirror onto SynologyUnc with optional DPAPI credential). Empty list = no sync, current behaviour. Per-target results land in <LibraryRoot>/.musicripper/sync-state.json.
//LocalRetention: Phase 6.1: what to do with the local album folder after every configured sync target reported OK. 'Keep' (default, never touch local files), 'MoveToSentAfterAllSynced' (move to <LibraryRoot>/_Sent/ preserving the artist subdir), or 'RecycleAfterAllSynced' (send to the Windows Recycle Bin, recoverable). No-op when SyncTargets is empty or any target failed.
//RetryPendingSyncOnStartup: Phase 6.5: at startup -- BEFORE any disc-rip -- show a WPF dialog that retries albums whose previous sync didn't finish (e.g. NAS was offline). Default true. Set false if you'd rather run src/tools/Sync-PendingAlbums.ps1 manually. The dialog has its own Cancel button so a single launch can still skip the retry without flipping this flag.
//WireGuardTunnelName: Phase 6.4: bare tunnel name (typically the .conf filename without extension) of a WireGuard tunnel installed via wireguard.exe /installtunnelservice. When set AND WireGuardAutoToggle=true, MusicRipper starts the tunnel before each NAS sync and stops it on application exit. The Windows service it manages is 'WireGuardTunnel$<Name>'. setup/New-RipperConfig.ps1 prompts for a .conf path and runs install + sc.exe sdset (one UAC prompt) so subsequent rips are silent. null = no VPN management; the share must already be reachable.
//WireGuardAutoToggle: Phase 6.4: master switch for tunnel management. true (default) = MusicRipper brings WireGuardTunnelName up around each NAS sync and tears it down when the sync is done. false = leave the tunnel state entirely to the user (e.g. always-on VPN, or you manage it via the WireGuard tray app yourself).
//WireGuardKeepAliveBetweenDiscs: Phase 6.4.1: false (default) = each NAS sync brings the tunnel up just for the duration of that sync (one robocopy invocation), so a 30-disc rip session bounces it 30 times. true = the first sync's start pins the tunnel up for the rest of the session and it's torn down at exit. Set to true if the ~2-3s WG re-handshake per disc adds up for your workflow; leave false if you want the tunnel open as briefly as possible.
